---
output: github_document 
---

# YouTube Data Analysis with R

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file  -->

take a look at my youtube playlist with R Tutorials: 

---

https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLRbCt61PaxX2d0_QXh6Qi6_jAQd66fmcI

---

## Project Overview

This is an **R project for YouTube Data API automation** that:
- Interacts with YouTube Data API v3 and YouTube Analytics API v2
- Manages OAuth authentication for `tuber` and `YTAnalytics` packages
- Extracts video metadata, statistics, captions from playlists
- Generates visualizations (bar plots, pie charts, dual-axis plotly graphs)
- Downloads YouTube videos using yt-dlp
- Uses local LLMs (via LM Studio) to summarize video captions in Portuguese

## Architecture

### Core Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `youtubeFunctions.R` | Contains all reusable functions for YouTube API operations |
| `youtube.01.stats.R` | Statistics workflow: channel info, playlists, video lists, plots |
| `youtube.02.captions.R` | Caption extraction and AI summarization with local LLMs |
| `youtube.03.downloads.R` | YouTube video/playlist downloads via yt-dlp |
| `SETUP.R` | Environment setup for RStudio sessions (starts LM Studio, Claude) |

### Function Categories

**Authentication:**
- `AUTHENTICATE_TUBER()` - OAuth for tuber package (channel stats, metadata)
- `AUTHENTICATE_YTANALYTICS()` - OAuth for YTAnalytics package (analytics data)

**Data Retrieval:**
- `GET_CHANNEL_VIDEOS(channel_id)` - List all channel videos
- `GET_CHANNEL_PLAYLISTS(channel_id, exclude_playlists)` - List playlists with filtering
- `GET_VIDEOS_FROM_PLAYLISTS(playlists)` - Extract metadata from playlist items
- `GET_CAPTIONS_FROM_PLAYLIST(playlist_id)` - Get caption tracks for playlist videos
- `MERGE_VIDEOS_WITH_CAPTIONS(videos, captions)` - Join captions to video data

**Analytics & Visualization:**
- `EVOLUTION_OF_MY_VIEWS(fromDate)` - Dual-axis plot of cumulative views vs uploads
- `PLOT_BARPLOT_VIDEOS_BY_PLAYLIST()` - Bar chart of videos per playlist
- `PLOT_PIECHART_VIDEOS_BY_PLAYLIST()` - Pie chart of videos per playlist
- `PLOT_THUMBNAILS(urls, ncol, nrow)` - Grid of video thumbnails

**Downloads:**
- `GET_YT_DLP_COMMAND(youtube_url, OutputFolder, url_type)` - Build yt-dlp command

**Specialized:**
- `PROCESS_INFINIFACTORY_VIDEOS()` - Extract block counts from Infinifactory videos
- `SANITIZE_FILENAME(file_name)` - Clean filenames for Windows compatibility

## Common Development Tasks

### Running the Project

```r
# Load all functions and run stats workflow
source("youtube.01.stats.R")

# Run captions workflow (requires LM Studio running)
source("youtube.02.captions.R")

# Run downloads workflow
source("youtube.03.downloads.R")
```

### Authentication Setup

Set environment variables in `.Renviron`:
```r
YOUTUBE_CLIENT_ID = "your_client_id"
YOUTUBE_CLIENT_SECRET = "your_client_secret"
YOUTUBE_CHANNEL_ID = "your_channel_id"
```

Or use Windows Credential Manager:
```r
keyring::key_set("YOUTUBE_CHANNEL_ID")
YOUTUBE_CHANNEL_ID = keyring::key_get("YOUTUBE_CHANNEL_ID")
```

### Running a Single Test

To test a specific function without loading everything:
```r
source("youtubeFunctions.R")
AUTHENTICATE_TUBER()  # or AUTHENTICATE_YTANALYTICS()
# Then call the function you want to test
```

## Important Notes

- **OAuth tokens** are stored in `.httr-oauth` - excluded from git via `.gitignore`
- Two separate OAuth directories exist: `tuber_oauth/` and `ytanalytics_oauth/`
- The project uses Portuguese for AI-generated summaries
- yt-dlp executable is located in `./tools/yt-dlp.exe`
- LM Studio must be running for caption summarization (started via SETUP.R)

## Package Dependencies

Core packages used:
- `tuber` - YouTube Data API v3 client
- `YTAnalytics` - YouTube Analytics API client
- `plotly` - Interactive visualizations
- `magick` - Image manipulation (thumbnails, avatars)
- `dplyr`, `lubridate` - Data manipulation
- `countrycode` - Country code conversion
- `keyring` - Credential management

Custom packages from GitHub:
- `xfunctions` - RStudio utilities
- `minifunctions` - Lightweight data printing
- `svDialogs` - User input dialogs
- `notifier` - Desktop notifications
- `zeallot` - Destructuring assignment
- `rstudiotools` - RStudio integration
